title: Friday, 07/08/2026 Quiz: The August Mix
description: A relaxed Friday quiz covering movies and television, pop culture, geography, and light current world politics. Political facts were checked on 17 July 2026 and should be rechecked before hosting.

questions.question: In Friends, what is the name of the coffee shop where the group regularly meets?
questions.notes: Central Perk is the café with the orange sofa.

questions.question: Which New Zealand filmmaker directed The Lord of the Rings film trilogy?
questions.notes: Accept Peter Jackson with or without his title.

questions.question: Who plays Wednesday Addams in the Netflix series Wednesday?
questions.notes: Jenna Ortega first played the role in the series released in 2022.

questions.question: What is the surname of the wealthy media family at the centre of Succession?
questions.notes: Accept Roy or the Roy family.

questions.question: In Breaking Bad, what alias does Walter White use in the drug trade?
questions.notes: The alias is Heisenberg, borrowed from physicist Werner Heisenberg.

questions.question: Who directed the 2023 film Barbie?
questions.notes: Greta Gerwig directed and co-wrote the film.

questions.question: What is the name of the town where The Simpsons live?
questions.notes: The state is deliberately left ambiguous, but the town is Springfield.

questions.question: In Stranger Things, what is the dark alternate dimension called?
questions.notes: Accept The Upside Down or simply Upside Down.

questions.question: In the US version of The Office, which company employs the main characters?
questions.notes: Dunder Mifflin is a fictional paper company.

questions.question: In Pixar's Inside Out, which emotion is represented by the blue character?
questions.notes: Sadness is blue and is voiced by Phyllis Smith in the English-language film.

questions.question: What nickname is commonly used for fans of Taylor Swift?
questions.notes: Accept Swifties or Swiftie.

questions.question: What is the title of Beyoncé's country-influenced 2024 album?
questions.notes: Texas Hold 'Em is a single from the album; the album is Cowboy Carter.

questions.question: Which South Korean performer had the worldwide hit Gangnam Style?
questions.notes: PSY released Gangnam Style in 2012.

questions.question: Which city hosted the 2024 Summer Olympic Games?
questions.notes: Paris hosted the Games, with some events held elsewhere in France and French Polynesia.

questions.question: Name the four members of The Beatles.
questions.notes: Award one point each for John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison, and Ringo Starr.

questions.question: Which singer released the album Brat, inspiring the bright-green 'brat summer' trend?
questions.notes: Brat was released by Charli XCX in 2024.

questions.question: Which rapper headlined the Super Bowl halftime show in February 2025?
questions.notes: Kendrick Lamar headlined Super Bowl LIX's halftime show.

questions.question: Which performer is widely nicknamed the 'Queen of Pop'?
questions.notes: Madonna is commonly known as the Queen of Pop.

questions.question: Which New Zealand singer became an international star with the song Royals?
questions.notes: Lorde is the stage name of Ella Yelich-O'Connor.

questions.question: What blended nickname was given to the shared 2023 cinema craze around Barbie and Oppenheimer?
questions.notes: The popular portmanteau was Barbenheimer.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Canada?
questions.notes: Ottawa is the capital; Toronto is Canada's largest city.

questions.question: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.notes: The Pacific Ocean is larger than all Earth's land area combined.

questions.question: Which strait separates New Zealand's North Island and South Island?
questions.notes: Cook Strait lies between Wellington and the Marlborough Sounds.

questions.question: Which continent is the only one with land in all four hemispheres?
questions.notes: Both the Equator and the Prime Meridian cross Africa.

questions.question: The island of New Guinea is divided between which two countries?
questions.notes: Award one point each for Papua New Guinea and Indonesia.

questions.question: Lake Titicaca lies on the border of which two countries?
questions.notes: Award one point each for Peru and Bolivia.

questions.question: Which New Zealand city is commonly known as the Garden City?
questions.notes: Christchurch is widely known as New Zealand's Garden City.

questions.question: Which famous river flows through Egypt and reaches the Mediterranean Sea?
questions.notes: Accept Nile or Nile River.

questions.question: Which European country is often described as being shaped like a boot?
questions.notes: The Italian peninsula creates the familiar boot shape.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.notes: Canberra, not Sydney or Melbourne, is Australia's capital.

questions.question: Which politician returned to the White House in January 2025 after previously serving as US president from 2017 to 2021?
questions.notes: Donald Trump is both the 45th and 47th US president. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Which party won a large majority in the United Kingdom's 2024 general election?
questions.notes: Labour won 411 seats and a majority in the House of Commons.

questions.question: Who became prime minister of Canada in March 2025, succeeding Justin Trudeau?
questions.notes: Mark Carney became Canada's 24th prime minister. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Claudia Sheinbaum became the first woman elected president of which country?
questions.notes: Claudia Sheinbaum took office as president of Mexico in October 2024.

questions.question: Giorgia Meloni is the prime minister of which European country?
questions.notes: Giorgia Meloni has led Italy's government since October 2022. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: Alongside the National Party, which two parties make up New Zealand's current coalition government?
questions.notes: Award one point each for ACT New Zealand and New Zealand First. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.

questions.question: What does NATO's Article 5 broadly mean?
answers: An attack on one member is treated as an attack on all
An armed attack on one member is considered an attack on all members
choices: An attack on one member is treated as an attack on all
Every member must spend five percent of GDP on defence
All military decisions require five votes
questions.notes: It is NATO's collective-defence commitment. Assistance does not automatically have to mean the use of armed force.

questions.question: Which country became NATO's newest member in 2024?
questions.notes: Sweden became NATO's 32nd member in March 2024; Finland joined in 2023.

questions.question: Which United Nations body has five permanent members with veto power?
questions.notes: The five permanent Security Council members are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

questions.question: Which political party does New Zealand prime minister Christopher Luxon lead?
questions.notes: Christopher Luxon is leader of the New Zealand National Party. Current as checked on 17 July 2026.
